description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: build-app and build-image. In the build-app stage add a job named "build app" that uses the node:12 image, caches the node_modules directory, and stores the build directory as an artifact that expires in 1 day. The job runs the scripts: "yarn", "yarn lint", "yarn generate-graphql-typings", a conditional check "if [ -n \"$(git status --porcelain)\" ]; then echo \"Please run \"yarn generate-graphql-typings\" and commit the result\"; exit 1; fi", and finally "yarn build". In the build-image stage add a job named "build image" that uses the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service, runs "docker info", logs in to the GitLab registry with CI variables, attempts to pull $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG or $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:master, builds the image with "docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA --cache-from=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME --cache-from=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:master --shm-size 512M .", pushes the SHA tag, tags it with the ref slug, and pushes that tag.
